title: little-loops Configuration
description: Configuration schema for little-loops development workflow plugin

categories.description: Issue category definitions. Required categories (bugs, features, enhancements, epics) are automatically included if not specified. Custom categories are merged with required ones.

status.description: Valid status values for issue frontmatter: open | in_progress | blocked | deferred | done | cancelled

completed_dir.description: [DEPRECATED: use IssueInfo.status instead] Directory for completed issues

deferred_dir.description: [DEPRECATED: use IssueInfo.status instead] Directory for deferred issues (parked, not active or completed)

capture_template.description: Creation variant for issues assembled by skills (capture-issue, format-issue, ready-issue)

exact_threshold.description: Jaccard similarity threshold above which a finding is treated as an exact duplicate (skip)

similar_threshold.description: Jaccard similarity threshold above which a finding is treated as similar (update existing issue)

next_issue.description: Selection behavior for ll-issues next-issue / next-issues commands

strategy.description: Named preset for sort ordering. 'confidence_first' (default) sorts by (-outcome_confidence, -confidence_score, priority_int); 'priority_first' sorts by (priority_int, -outcome_confidence, -confidence_score). Custom orderings use sort_keys instead.

sort_keys.description: Optional custom sort order overriding strategy. Each entry specifies a sortable IssueInfo field and direction.

parent.description: Parent issue ID (e.g., EPIC-123 or ENH-100)

blocked_by.description: List of issue IDs that block this issue

depends_on.description: List of issue IDs this issue depends on (soft prerequisite)

relates_to.description: List of issue IDs related to this issue

duplicate_of.description: Issue ID that this issue duplicates

labels.description: Cross-cutting classification labels (e.g., fsm, cli, quick-win)

milestone.description: Sprint or milestone name this issue is assigned to (e.g., sprint-2026-q2)

auto_commit.description: Automatically commit issue file changes after CRUD operations

auto_commit_prefix.description: Commit message prefix used by the auto-commit hook

idle_timeout_seconds.description: Seconds of inactivity before automation considers the session idle. 0 disables.

max_continuations.description: Maximum number of continuation prompts before automation stops.

parallel.description: Parallel automation settings with git worktree isolation (ll-parallel)

p0_sequential.description: Process P0 issues sequentially (not in parallel)

state_file.description: State file for parallel processing resume

max_merge_retries.description: Maximum rebase attempts before failing merge

stream_subprocess_output.description: Stream Claude CLI output to console

decide_command.description: Command template for the decide-issue step. {{issue_id}} is substituted at runtime.

worktree_copy_files.description: Additional files to copy from main repo to worktrees (relative paths). Includes .claude/settings.local.json by default because it carries Claude Code auth tokens required in each worktree. Note: .claude/ directory is always copied automatically.

require_code_changes.description: Require worktree to produce code changes before merging. Skips no-op runs.

use_feature_branches.description: Create a dedicated feature branch per issue (e.g., feature/ENH-123-slug) instead of the default parallel/<id>-<timestamp> branch. When true, auto-merge to main is skipped and branches survive as PR-ready. Use for PR-based CI/CD workflows.

remote_name.description: Git remote name used for fetch/pull operations in parallel runs. Use if your remote is not named 'origin'.

confidence_gate.description: Confidence score gate for manage-issue implementation phase

enabled.description: Enable confidence score gate before implementation

readiness_threshold.description: Minimum confidence_score (readiness, 0-100) required to proceed with implementation

outcome_threshold.description: Minimum outcome_confidence (0-100) required to proceed with implementation

tdd_mode.description: Enable TDD mode: write failing tests before implementation in manage-issue

max_refine_count.description: Maximum total lifetime /ll:refine-issue full-rewrite calls allowed per issue. Gap-analysis runs (--gap-analysis flag) are exempt from this cap. Enforced by the refine-to-ready-issue loop and directly by check_attempt_budget in recursive-refine before each sub-loop entry.

rate_limits.description: Global rate-limit resilience defaults applied when a state does not override per-state rate-limit fields, plus shared circuit-breaker coordination settings.

max_wait_seconds.description: Total wall-clock budget (seconds) for rate-limit handling before routing to on_rate_limit_exhausted.

long_wait_ladder.description: Backoff ladder (seconds) for the long-wait tier used once the short-tier retry budget is spent.

circuit_breaker_enabled.description: Enable the shared rate-limit circuit breaker across parallel worktrees.

circuit_breaker_path.description: Path (relative to project root) for the shared circuit-breaker state file.

stale_days.description: Days without activity before a child issue is considered stalled

enable_scope_drift_check.description: Enable LLM-based scope-drift and missing-coverage passes

enabled.description: Enable the decisions and rules log

log_path.description: Path to the decisions YAML log file

auto_generate.description: Entry IDs to auto-generate on init

focus_dirs.description: Directories to focus on during scans

exclude_patterns.description: Glob patterns to exclude from scans

mode.description: Optimization mode (quick uses config only, thorough spawns agent)

confirm.description: Show diff and ask for confirmation before applying

bypass_prefix.description: Prefix to bypass optimization (e.g., * to skip)

clarity_threshold.description: Minimum clarity score (1-10) to pass through unchanged

include_todos.description: Include todo list state in continuation prompt

include_git_status.description: Include git status in continuation prompt

include_recent_files.description: Include recently modified files in continuation prompt

max_continuations.description: Maximum automatic session continuations for CLI tools

prompt_expiry_hours.description: Hours before continuation prompt is considered stale

context_monitor.description: Proactive context monitoring with automatic handoff before hitting limits

enabled.description: Enable proactive context monitoring with auto-handoff

auto_handoff_threshold.description: Estimated context usage percentage to trigger automatic handoff (0-100)

estimate_weights.description: Weight factors for context estimation heuristics

tool_call_base.description: Base tokens per tool call overhead

bash_output_per_char.description: Estimated tokens per character of bash output

per_turn_overhead.description: Tokens added per tool call to account for Claude output and user message tokens

system_prompt_baseline.description: One-time token estimate for system prompt (CLAUDE.md, skills, etc.) added on first tool call

context_limit_estimate.description: Override for the context window token limit. Omit or set to 0 for auto-detection (known claude-*-4* variants -> 200000; if transcript baseline exceeds that, auto-upgrades to 1000000). Set to an explicit non-zero value to override, e.g. 1000000 for 1M-context models.

state_file.description: File to track context usage state

post_compaction_percent.description: After context compaction, reset token estimate to this percentage of context_limit_estimate as a safety margin

use_transcript_baseline.description: Use JSONL transcript usage fields as accurate baseline for token estimation (one-turn lag). Falls back to pure heuristics if transcript is unavailable.

scratch_pad.description: Observation masking via scratch pad files to reduce context bloat in automation sessions (disabled by default)

enabled.description: Enable scratch pad instructions for automation sessions

threshold_lines.description: Line count threshold above which tool outputs are redirected to scratch files

automation_contexts_only.description: Only enforce scratch pad redirection in automation sessions (ll-auto, ll-parallel, ll-sprint); skip in interactive sessions

tail_lines.description: Number of lines to surface via tail when redirecting large outputs to a scratch file

command_allowlist.description: Shell commands eligible for scratch pad redirection by the PreToolUse hook

file_extension_filters.description: File extensions eligible for scratch pad redirection when read by tooling

documents.description: Key document tracking by category for issue alignment validation

product.description: Product/business analysis configuration (opt-in feature, disabled by default)

analyze_user_impact.description: Include user impact assessment in issues

analyze_business_value.description: Include business value scoring in issues

goals_discovery.description: Settings for auto-discovering product goals from documentation

max_files.description: Maximum markdown files to analyze for goal discovery

required_files.description: Files that must exist for discovery (warning if missing)

default_timeout.description: Default timeout per issue in seconds

default_max_workers.description: Default worker count for parallel execution within waves

loops_dir.description: Directory for loop definitions and runtime state

queue_wait_timeout_seconds.description: Seconds to wait for a conflicting scope lock to be released when --queue is used.

glyphs.description: Override unicode badge glyphs shown in FSM box diagrams. Omitted keys use built-in defaults.

enabled.description: Master switch for the Learning Test Registry. When false, all LT-related surfaces (discoverability hook, gate-loop hints) are silenced. Set via /ll:init or /ll:configure.

stale_after_days.description: Number of days after which a learning test record is considered stale

discoverability.description: Controls how learning-test gaps are surfaced during implementation.

refine_status.description: Display settings for the ll-issues refine-status command

columns.description: Ordered list of columns to display in refine-status table output. Valid names: id, priority, size, title, source, norm, fmt, ready, confidence, score_complexity, score_test_coverage, score_ambiguity, score_change_surface, total. Unknown names render as —. Omit or leave empty to use the default column set.

elide_order.description: Ordered list of columns to drop (first to last) when the table exceeds terminal width. Columns absent from this list are treated as pinned and never elided. id, priority, and title are always pinned by the implementation. Command columns not listed are dropped rightmost-first after this list is exhausted. Omit or leave empty to use the default elision order.

dependency_mapping.description: Dependency mapping threshold configuration for overlap detection and conflict scoring

overlap_min_files.description: Minimum overlapping files to trigger overlap detection

overlap_min_ratio.description: Minimum ratio of overlapping files to smaller set

min_directory_depth.description: Minimum path segments for directory overlap (e.g., src/components/ = 2)

conflict_threshold.description: Conflict score cutoff: below = parallel-safe, above = dependency proposed

high_conflict_threshold.description: Conflict score above which issues are labeled HIGH conflict

confidence_modifier.description: Confidence reduction applied when dependency direction is ambiguous

scoring_weights.description: Weights for semantic conflict scoring signals (should sum to 1.0)

semantic.description: Weight for semantic target overlap (component/function names)

section.description: Weight for section mention overlap (UI regions)

exclude_common_files.description: Infrastructure files excluded from overlap detection

sync.description: GitHub Issues synchronization settings (opt-in feature, disabled by default)

provider.description: Issue tracking provider (currently only GitHub supported)

repo.description: GitHub repository (owner/repo format, auto-detected if null)

label_mapping.description: Map issue types to GitHub labels

sync_completed.description: Also sync completed issues (close on GitHub)

pull_template.description: Creation variant for issues pulled from GitHub (determines section structure)

color.description: Emit ANSI color codes in CLI output. Set to false for CI or plain-text environments. Also suppressed when the NO_COLOR environment variable is set.

colors.description: Override individual ANSI SGR color codes. Values are raw SGR parameter strings (e.g. "32", "38;5;208"). Unspecified keys retain their defaults.

info.description: ANSI SGR code for info messages (default: cyan)

success.description: ANSI SGR code for success messages (default: green)

warning.description: ANSI SGR code for warning messages (default: yellow)

error.description: ANSI SGR code for error messages (default: orange)

P0.description: ANSI SGR code for P0 (default: bold orange)

P1.description: ANSI SGR code for P1 (default: orange)

P2.description: ANSI SGR code for P2 (default: yellow)

P3.description: ANSI SGR code for P3 (default: normal)

P4.description: ANSI SGR code for P4 (default: dim)

P5.description: ANSI SGR code for P5 (default: dim)

type.description: Colors for issue type labels (BUG, FEAT, ENH, EPIC).

BUG.description: ANSI SGR code for BUG labels (default: orange)

FEAT.description: ANSI SGR code for FEAT labels (default: green)

ENH.description: ANSI SGR code for ENH labels (default: blue)

EPIC.description: ANSI SGR code for EPIC labels (default: magenta)

fsm_active_state.description: ANSI SGR code for the active FSM state highlight during verbose run (default: green)

fsm_edge_labels.description: ANSI SGR color overrides for FSM transition edge labels in loop diagrams.

yes.description: ANSI SGR code for 'yes' transitions (default: green)

no.description: ANSI SGR code for 'no' transitions (default: orange)

error.description: ANSI SGR code for 'error' transitions (default: red)

partial.description: ANSI SGR code for 'partial' transitions (default: yellow)

next.description: ANSI SGR code for 'next' transitions (default: dim)

default.description: ANSI SGR code for unlabeled ('_') transitions (default: dim)

blocked.description: ANSI SGR code for 'blocked' transitions (default: red)

retry_exhausted.description: ANSI SGR code for 'retry_exhausted' transitions (default: orange)

rate_limit_exhausted.description: ANSI SGR code for 'rate_limit_exhausted' transitions (default: amber)

extensions.description: Extension module paths to load (format: 'module.path:ClassName'). Extensions receive structured events from the EventBus.

hooks.description: Hook-intent abstraction layer configuration (FEAT-1116). Per-host adapters parse the host's native hook payload into LLHookEvent and invoke host-agnostic intent handlers under little_loops.hooks.

host.description: Host agent identifier. Used by adapters to translate between the host's native hook protocol and the LLHookEvent/LLHookResult wire format.

stale_ref_fix.description: Session-end stale-ref sweep mode (FEAT-1680). report prints findings to stderr; auto also rewrites them in-place.

min_children.description: Minimum EPIC children. Fewer proposals triggers a warning to consider /ll:capture-issue instead.

max_children.description: Maximum EPIC children. More proposals triggers a sub-EPIC decomposition suggestion.

cascade.description: Cascade settings for EPIC status transitions.

default_status.description: Default status applied to children when cascading EPIC closure via --cascade (without explicit --cascade-to).

events.description: Event transport configuration. Transports are sinks attached to the EventBus and receive every emitted event.

transports.description: Names of transports to wire onto the EventBus (e.g. "jsonl", "socket"). Unknown names are skipped with a warning.

socket.description: UnixSocketTransport configuration. Streams newline-delimited JSON events over an AF_UNIX socket for sub-second latency local consumers (TUIs, log tailers). Unavailable on Windows.

path.description: Filesystem path for the AF_UNIX socket.

max_clients.description: Maximum simultaneous client connections; further connects are rejected at accept time.

otel.description: OTelTransport configuration. Maps loop executions to OpenTelemetry traces/spans and exports via OTLP. Requires pip install 'little-loops[otel]'.

endpoint.description: OTLP gRPC endpoint for the collector (Grafana Agent, Jaeger, Datadog, etc.).

service_name.description: OpenTelemetry service.name resource attribute applied to all emitted spans.

webhook.description: WebhookTransport configuration. POSTs batched events to an HTTP endpoint for remote dashboards, Slack bots, and CI systems. Requires pip install 'little-loops[webhooks]'.

url.description: HTTP endpoint to POST batched events to. Set to null to disable the webhook transport even if 'webhook' is listed in transports.

batch_ms.description: Flush interval in milliseconds. Events accumulate in memory and are POSTed as a JSON array on each tick.

headers.description: Additional HTTP headers sent with every POST (e.g. {"Authorization": "Bearer token"}).

sqlite.description: SQLiteTransport configuration. Records FSM loop events and issue lifecycle events (issue.completed, issue.deferred, issue.skipped, issue.started, issue.closed, issue.failure_captured) into the per-project session store (.ll/history.db) for indexed cross-cutting queries via the ll-session CLI. Note: AutoManager.__init__() wires SQLiteTransport directly for ll-auto runs without requiring events.transports config.

path.description: Filesystem path for the SQLite session database.

orchestration.description: Orchestration settings, primarily host CLI selection. Values here are read by apply_host_cli_from_config() before resolve_host() runs.

host_cli.description: Override the host CLI used by orchestration scripts. Mirrors the LL_HOST_CLI environment variable; env var takes precedence if both are set.

composer.description: Settings for the loop-composer built-in orchestration loop.

max_plan_nodes.description: Maximum number of steps allowed in a single loop-composer plan. Plans exceeding this cap are rejected during validation.

auto.description: When true, skip the HITL plan-approval gate and execute the plan immediately. Defaults to false (require human approval) given the blast radius of multi-loop execution.

adaptive.description: Tuning knobs for the adaptive loop-composer-adaptive built-in loop.

enabled.description: When true, prefer the adaptive composer variant.

reassess_min_confidence.description: Confidence threshold below which the reassess gate triggers a re-plan.

cluster.description: Settings for the goal-cluster multi-goal orchestration loop.

max_batch_size.description: Maximum number of issues to process in a single cluster batch.

enable_dedup.description: When true, deduplicate issues with overlapping goals before batching.

propagate_context.description: When true, pass accumulated context from completed issues to subsequent batches.

design_tokens.description: Design system tokens consumed by built-in artifact-generating loops

enabled.description: Enable design token loading. When true, load_design_tokens() resolves the token path on session start.

path.description: Directory containing primitives, semantic, and themes files.

primitives_file.description: Filename for primitive token values within the path directory.

semantic_file.description: Filename for semantic token aliases within the path directory.

themes_dir.description: Subdirectory containing per-theme JSON override files.

active_theme.description: Name of the active theme file (without .json extension) under themes_dir.

active.description: Name of the active profile under profiles_dir (ENH-1768). Loader resolves token files from <path>/<profiles_dir>/<active>/. Falls back to the legacy flat layout (<path>/{primitives.json,...}) when no profile directory exists, so pre-ENH-1768 projects keep working.

profiles_dir.description: Subdirectory of <path> that contains profile directories (ENH-1768). Defaults to 'profiles' when null. Each profile is a self-contained token set (primitives, semantic, typography, spacing, themes/) selected via 'active'.

analytics.description: Context-window analytics settings (FEAT-1160). When enabled, the post_tool_use hook persists per-tool byte metrics into .ll/history.db for consumption by /ll:ctx-stats.

enabled.description: Enable per-tool byte tracking and file-event recording in post_tool_use hook. Default off; opt-in until the ctx-stats CLI ships.

capture.description: Controls which tool invocations and event types are captured into .ll/history.db (ENH-1840).

skills.description: Glob patterns for skill names to capture. Default ['*'] captures all skills.

cli_commands.description: Glob patterns for CLI command names to capture. Default ['*'] captures all commands.

corrections.description: Whether to capture user correction events.

correction_patterns.description: Additional regex patterns appended to the built-in correction detector. Built-ins always remain active. Patterns are raw regex strings (same style as _PHRASE_RE).

retention.description: Retention policy for history.db raw event tables (ENH-1906). Pruning is dual-gated: both min_project_age_days and min_db_size_mb must be exceeded before any rows are deleted. Default thresholds are generous so fresh or small projects are unaffected.

min_project_age_days.description: Minimum project age in days (measured as MIN(started_at) from sessions table). Both gates must be exceeded before pruning runs.

min_db_size_mb.description: Minimum DB file size in MB before pruning runs. Both gates must be exceeded.

raw_event_max_age_days.description: Delete tool_events, cli_events, file_events, message_events rows older than N days. Null disables per-table pruning. High-value tables (issue_events, user_corrections) are never pruned.

history.description: History.db read/consume configuration (ENH-1913). Single namespace owner for all history.db consumer tunables.

velocity_window.description: Number of recent issues to use when computing velocity (ENH-1905).

effort_fields.description: Fields extracted from history.db for effort reporting (ENH-1905).

max_age_days.description: Maximum age in days for history entries; null = no limit (ENH-1905).

planning_skills.description: Skill names whose sessions are included in planning context queries (ENH-1909).

enabled.description: Enable session digest injection into skill prompts.

days.description: Number of days of session history to include in digests.

char_cap.description: Maximum characters for the digest block.

sections.description: Ordered list of digest section providers to include. Empty list = all v1 providers.

feedback_min_recurrence.description: Minimum recurrence count for a correction to surface in evolution analysis.

bypass_min_count.description: Minimum bypass count threshold for evolution signal suppression.

correction_penalty.description: Score penalty applied per correction event in go/no-go scoring.

dup_overlap_threshold.description: Overlap ratio threshold above which a new issue is considered a duplicate.

compaction.description: LCM-style compaction configuration for summary_nodes (FEAT-1712). Controls a three-level LCM Algorithm 3 summarization pipeline (normal LLM → aggressive bullet-point LLM → deterministic truncation) over message_events blocks, with guaranteed convergence.

enabled.description: Enable LLM-based session compaction during backfill. Disabled by default to avoid background LLM calls without opt-in.

budget_tokens.description: Token budget per summary leaf block. Token estimate: len(content) // 4.

model.description: Model override for compaction LLM calls. Null means use the host default.

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds for each LLM compaction call. On timeout, escalation proceeds through remaining levels, ultimately to deterministic truncation.

cross_session_enabled.description: Enable recursive cross-session condensation after per-session compaction (ENH-1954). When true, existing condensed nodes are grouped level-by-level and summarised until a single project-root summary node remains. Set to false to preserve pre-ENH-1954 behavior.

max_level.description: Maximum condensation depth. Null means no limit — the pass recurses until exactly one root node remains. Set to an integer to stop after that many cross-session levels.
